stem: One gallon of varnish will cover 140 square feet of a surface. A deck has a total surface area of w square feet. Which equation represents the total amount of varnish V, in gallons, needed to varnish the deck twice?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. One coat requires w/140 gallons; two coats require 2w/140 gallons.

Choice A inverts the relationship. Choice B inverts both quantities. Choice C uses the wrong denominator (280 instead of 140), corresponding to single coverage at half the rate.
